overview: Murders at Karlov Manor is a mystery-themed set set on Ravnica. The format centers around the Collect Evidence and Suspect mechanics, creating a unique interplay between graveyard management and combat. The format is medium-paced with strong removal and rewards players who can efficiently manage their resources.

mechanics.name: Collect Evidence
mechanics.description: To Collect Evidence N, exile cards with total mana value N or greater from your graveyard.
mechanics.draft_tip: Collect Evidence needs graveyard fuel. Prioritize trades and self-mill to stock your graveyard. Higher mana value cards in the graveyard are more efficient fuel.

mechanics.name: Suspect
mechanics.description: A suspected creature has menace and can't block. You can suspect your own creatures for offensive pressure.
mechanics.draft_tip: Suspecting your own creatures is often correct for pushing damage. The menace is powerful offensively, but remember they can't block — don't suspect creatures you need for defense.

mechanics.name: Disguise
mechanics.description: You may cast a card with Disguise face down as a 2/2 creature with ward 2 for 3 mana. Turn it face up any time for its disguise cost.
mechanics.draft_tip: Disguise creatures are flexible — they curve out as 2/2s and flip up for value later. The ward 2 makes them surprisingly resilient. Plan your mana for the flip cost.

mechanics.name: Cloak
mechanics.description: Put a card from a specified zone face down as a 2/2 creature with ward 2. If it's a creature card, it can be turned face up for its mana cost.
mechanics.draft_tip: Cloak effects are great value generators. Even non-creature cards become 2/2 bodies with ward 2, which is relevant board presence.

mechanics.name: Cases
mechanics.description: Case enchantments have three parts: an initial effect, a condition to solve, and a solved bonus.
mechanics.draft_tip: Cases that are easy to solve are much better than ones with difficult conditions. Evaluate them primarily on the initial effect — the solved bonus is gravy.

archetypes.name: Disguise Tempo
archetypes.description: White-Blue uses Disguise creatures and tempo plays to control the pace of the game.
archetypes.strategy: Deploy Disguise creatures early, then flip them at key moments for tempo. Use bounce and removal to stay ahead on board.

archetypes.name: Collect Evidence Control
archetypes.description: Blue-Black uses Collect Evidence to fuel powerful spells and grind out advantages.
archetypes.strategy: Trade creatures early to fuel the graveyard. Use Collect Evidence payoffs and removal to control the game and win in the late game.

archetypes.name: Suspect Aggro
archetypes.description: Black-Red uses Suspect to give creatures menace and push through damage aggressively.
archetypes.strategy: Suspect your creatures and attack with menace. Use removal to clear the most important blockers and close games fast.

archetypes.name: Cluetokens Midrange
archetypes.description: Red-Green creates and sacrifices Clue tokens for value while deploying efficient threats.
archetypes.strategy: Generate Clues for card advantage while playing efficient midrange threats. Use the extra cards to maintain pressure.

archetypes.name: Disguise Midrange
archetypes.description: Green-White deploys Disguise creatures and uses efficient creatures to dominate the mid-game.
archetypes.strategy: Curve Disguise creatures into powerful flips. Use Green's size and White's removal to control combat.

archetypes.name: Evidence Removal
archetypes.description: White-Black combines premium removal with Collect Evidence value plays.
archetypes.strategy: Draft premium removal highly. Use Collect Evidence to power up your spells while creatures handle the win condition.

archetypes.name: Clues Tempo
archetypes.description: Blue-Red generates Clue tokens and uses spells-matter synergies for tempo.
archetypes.strategy: Generate Clues for cards while using tempo spells. Crack Clues to find answers and maintain pressure.

archetypes.name: Graveyard Value
archetypes.description: Black-Green fills the graveyard and uses it as a resource for Collect Evidence and recursion.
archetypes.strategy: Self-mill and trade early. Convert graveyard resources into powerful late-game plays with Collect Evidence.

archetypes.name: Aggro
archetypes.description: Red-White plays the most straightforward aggressive strategy with efficient creatures and burn.
archetypes.strategy: Curve low and attack. Use burn and removal to clear blockers and close games before opponents stabilize.

archetypes.name: Cloak Ramp
archetypes.description: Green-Blue uses Cloak effects and ramp to deploy threats ahead of curve.
archetypes.strategy: Ramp and Cloak cards for board presence. Flip creatures at key moments for tempo swings.

general_tips: Disguise creatures with ward 2 are surprisingly hard to remove efficiently. Don't underestimate face-down 2/2s.
Collect Evidence gets better as the game goes long. Plan your graveyard usage — don't exile everything for a small payoff.
Suspect is a powerful offensive tool but leaves you vulnerable on defense. Only Suspect when you're the aggressor.
Cases are usually worth playing even if you never solve them — evaluate based on the initial effect.
The format has excellent removal across all colors. Don't over-invest in a single threat.
Clue tokens provide card advantage but cost mana to crack. Balance card advantage with tempo.
